title: In Memory of Walter Savage Landor by Algernon Charles Swinburne — Sad Poetry Lines
content:
Back to the flower-town, side by side,
The bright months bring,
New-born, the bridegroom and the bride,
Freedom and spring.
The sweet land laughs from sea to sea,
Filled full of sun;
All things come back to her, being free;
All things but one.
meaning: "In Memory of Walter Savage Landor" by Algernon Charles Swinburne is a sad and love and deep and nature and inspirational poem consisting of 62 lines. The poet explores themes of sad and love and deep and nature and inspirational through vivid imagery and emotional depth. Beginning with "Back to the flower-town, side by side, The bright months bring,...", this piece captures the essence of human experience and invites reflection on the deeper currents of life and feeling. This work is part of Algernon Charles Swinburne's celebrated body of poetry that continues to resonate with readers worldwide.
